data.content:
### Copy SSH Public Key to Remote Host

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/remote-gateway-readme.md

This command copies your local SSH public key to the remote machine, enabling passwordless SSH authentication. You will be prompted for the remote user's password once during this process. Ensure your public key (`~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub`) exists before running this command.

```bash
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_rsa <REMOTE_USER>@<REMOTE_IP>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure SSH Client for Remote Gateway

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/remote-gateway-readme.md

This configuration snippet for `~/.ssh/config` sets up a host alias 'remote-gateway' to manage SSH connections to a remote server. It includes specifying the hostname, username, local port forwarding, and the identity file for authentication. Ensure you replace placeholders with your actual remote IP and username.

```sshconfig
Host remote-gateway
    HostName <REMOTE_IP>          # e.g., 172.27.187.184
    User <REMOTE_USER>            # e.g., jefferson
    LocalForward 18789 127.0.0.1:18789
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa
```

--------------------------------

### Write Moltbot Config via SSH (echo/tee)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Write a custom configuration file to '/data/moltbot.json' on the Moltbot instance using SSH. This method pipes JSON content from the local environment to the 'tee' command on the remote machine.

```bash
echo '{"your":"config"}' | fly ssh console -C "tee /data/moltbot.json"
```

--------------------------------

### Run Remote Moltbot Command (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/mac/remote.md

Example of how to send a notification to a remote Moltbot node using the CLI. This command requires the 'moltbot' CLI to be installed and accessible on the remote host.

```bash
moltbot nodes notify --node <id> --title "Ping" --body "Remote gateway ready" --sound Glass
```

--------------------------------

### Convert Moltbot to Private Deployment

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Transition an existing Moltbot deployment to a private configuration by releasing public IPs and then deploying with the private template. This ensures future deployments do not re-allocate public IPs.

```bash
# List current IPs
fly ips list -a my-moltbot

# Release public IPs
fly ips release <public-ipv4> -a my-moltbot
fly ips release <public-ipv6> -a my-moltbot

# Switch to private config so future deploys don't re-allocate public IPs
# (remove [http_service] or deploy with the private template)
fly deploy -c fly.private.toml

# Allocate private-only IPv6
fly ips allocate-v6 --private -a my-moltbot
```

--------------------------------

### Access Private Moltbot Deployment via Local Proxy

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Access a privately deployed Moltbot application by forwarding a local port to the application's internal port using 'fly proxy'. Once proxied, the application can be accessed via 'localhost' in a web browser.

```bash
# Forward local port 3000 to the app
fly proxy 3000:3000 -a my-moltbot

# Then open http://localhost:3000 in browser
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Private Moltbot Deployment via WireGuard

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Set up access to a privately deployed Moltbot application using a WireGuard VPN. This involves creating a WireGuard configuration using 'fly wireguard create' and then accessing the application via its internal IPv6 address.

```bash
# Create WireGuard config (one-time)
fly wireguard create

# Import to WireGuard client, then access via internal IPv6
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Moltbot Gateway Remote Mode

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/help/faq.md

This JSON configuration snippet demonstrates how to set Moltbot Gateway to remote mode, specifying the WebSocket URL and optional authentication credentials.

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "mode": "remote",
    "remote": {
      "url": "ws://gateway.tailnet:18789",
      "token": "your-token",
      "password": "your-password"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Moltbot on Fly.io (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

This command initiates the deployment of the Moltbot application to Fly.io. The first deployment may take a few minutes to build the Docker image. Subsequent deployments are typically faster.

```bash
fly deploy
```

--------------------------------

### Add Private Remote Repository (GitHub CLI)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md

Instructions for creating and adding a private remote repository using the GitHub CLI (`gh`). This command handles authentication, repository creation, and pushing the current directory as a private repository named 'clawd-workspace' to the 'origin' remote.

```bash
gh auth login
gh repo create clawd-workspace --private --source . --remote origin --push
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Moltbot Gateway for Remote Mode (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/configuration.md

This configuration example demonstrates setting up the Moltbot gateway in 'remote' mode. It specifies the WebSocket URL for remote connections, along with authentication tokens and passwords required for remote CLI access.

```json5
{
  gateway: {
    mode: "remote",
    remote: {
      url: "ws://gateway.tailnet:18789",
      token: "your-token",
      password: "your-password"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Moltbot Gateway Remote Mode with Direct Transport (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/configuration.md

This JSON5 snippet illustrates configuring the Moltbot gateway for remote access using the 'direct' transport method. It specifies a secure WebSocket (wss) URL and an authentication token for establishing the remote connection from a macOS application.

```json5
{
  gateway: {
    mode: "remote",
    remote: {
      transport: "direct",
      url: "wss://gateway.example.ts.net",
      token: "your-token"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Oracle CLI Remote Browser Host Setup

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/skills/oracle/SKILL.md

Commands for setting up a remote browser host for the Oracle CLI and connecting to it from a client. This allows for distributed or remote execution of browser-based tasks.

```bash
# Host:
oracle serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9473 --token <secret>

# Client:
oracle --engine browser --remote-host <host:port> --remote-token <secret> -p "<task>" --file "src/**"
```

--------------------------------

### Run Command on Remote Node (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/tools/index.md

This snippet demonstrates how to execute a command on a remote node using the 'run' action. It specifies the command, environment variables, and various timeout settings. The 'needsScreenRecording' flag indicates if screen recording is required for the command execution.

```json
{
  "action": "run",
  "node": "office-mac",
  "command": ["echo", "Hello"],
  "env": ["FOO=bar"],
  "commandTimeoutMs": 12000,
  "invokeTimeoutMs": 45000,
  "needsScreenRecording": false
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set Moltbot Gateway Token

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/remote-gateway-readme.md

This command sets an environment variable `CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_TOKEN` on the client machine. This token is used by Moltbot.app to authenticate with the remote gateway. Replace `<your-token>` with the actual token provided for your gateway.

```bash
launchctl setenv CLAWDBOT_GATEWAY_TOKEN "<your-token>"
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Moltbot with Private Configuration

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Deploy Moltbot using a private configuration file ('fly.private.toml') to avoid public IP allocation and internet exposure. This is suitable for applications that only make outbound calls or use external tunnels for callbacks.

```bash
fly deploy -c fly.private.toml
```

--------------------------------

### Add Private Remote Repository (GitHub Web UI)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md

Instructions for adding a private remote repository using the GitHub web interface. This involves creating a new private repository, copying its HTTPS URL, and then using Git commands to add the remote and push the main branch. It's recommended not to initialize with a README to avoid merge conflicts.

```bash
git branch -M main
git remote add origin <https-url>
git push -u origin main
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Browserless Remote CDP Profile

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/tools/browser.md

This JSON configuration sets up a Moltbot browser profile to connect to a Browserless.io hosted Chromium service. It specifies the remote CDP URL, including authentication with an API key, and customizes timeouts and color for the profile.

```json
{
  "browser": {
    "enabled": true,
    "defaultProfile": "browserless",
    "remoteCdpTimeoutMs": 2000,
    "remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs": 4000,
    "profiles": {
      "browserless": {
        "cdpUrl": "https://production-sfo.browserless.io?token=<BROWSERLESS_API_KEY>",
        "color": "#00AA00"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start SSH Tunnel Manually

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/gateway/remote-gateway-readme.md

This command initiates an SSH tunnel in the background (`-N` flag prevents executing remote commands, `&` runs it in the background). It uses the 'remote-gateway' configuration defined in `~/.ssh/config` to establish the tunnel. This is a temporary setup and will stop if the terminal session ends.

```bash
ssh -N remote-gateway &
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Moltbot Gateway Remote Access (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/cli/acp.md

Sets Moltbot configuration for remote Gateway access. These commands persist the Gateway URL and authentication token, which can then be used by the 'moltbot acp' command without needing to specify them as flags.

```bash
moltbot config set gateway.remote.url wss://gateway-host:18789
moltbot config set gateway.remote.token <token>
```

--------------------------------

### Update Moltbot Application

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Update the Moltbot application by pulling the latest changes from the Git repository, deploying the new version, and then checking the application's status and logs.

```bash
# Pull latest changes
git pull

# Redeploy
fly deploy

# Check health
fly status
fly logs
```

--------------------------------

### Access Moltbot Gateway via SSH Tunnel

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/gcp.md

Establishes an SSH tunnel from the local machine to the Moltbot gateway running on a remote server. This command forwards the gateway's port (defaulting to 18789) from the remote server's loopback interface to the local machine, allowing access through `http://127.0.0.1:18789/`.

```bash
gcloud compute ssh moltbot-gateway --zone=us-central1-a -- -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789
```

--------------------------------

### Access Moltbot SSH Console

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/fly.md

Connect to the Moltbot application's SSH console for direct interaction and debugging. This command opens an interactive shell within the running container.

```bash
fly ssh console
```

--------------------------------

### Connect via SSH to Raspberry Pi

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/raspberry-pi.md

Connect to your Raspberry Pi remotely using SSH. This allows you to manage the device and install software from another computer on your network. You can use either the hostname or the IP address.

```bash
ssh user@gateway-host
# or use the IP address
ssh user@192.168.x.x
```

--------------------------------

### Establish SSH Tunnel for Moltbot Access

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/oracle.md

Use this SSH command to create a tunnel for accessing the Moltbot Control UI if Tailscale Serve is not functioning. This forwards a local port to the Moltbot instance's port, allowing access via localhost.

```bash
# From your local machine (via Tailscale)
ssh -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 ubuntu@moltbot
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Connect Tailscale on VPS and Mac

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/help/faq.md

Steps to install Tailscale on a Virtual Private Server (VPS) and a Mac, and connect them to the same tailnet. This enables secure communication between the devices using Tailscale's private networking.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh
sudo tailscale up
```

--------------------------------

### Proxy macOS Binaries via SSH for Linux Execution

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/help/faq.md

This snippet demonstrates how to create an SSH wrapper for a macOS binary to be executed from a Linux host. It involves creating a bash script that uses SSH to run the binary on a remote Mac and then overriding the skill metadata to allow Linux execution. This is an advanced method for running macOS-only skills on Linux.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
exec ssh -T user@mac-host /opt/homebrew/bin/imsg "$@"
```

```markdown
---
name: imsg
description: iMessage/SMS CLI for listing chats, history, watch, and sending.
metadata: {"moltbot":{"os":["darwin","linux"],"requires":{"bins":["imsg"]}}}
---
```

--------------------------------

### Remote iMessage Configuration with SSH and Attachments (JSON5)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/channels/imessage.md

This configuration sets up iMessage support on a remote Mac via SSH and enables automatic fetching of attachments. It specifies the SSH wrapper script, the remote host for SCP transfers, and a flag to include attachments.

```json
{
  channels: {
    imessage: {
      cliPath: "~/imsg-ssh",                     // SSH wrapper to remote Mac
      remoteHost: "user@gateway-host",           // for SCP file transfer
      includeAttachments: true
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Establish Local Port Forwarding

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/platforms/hetzner.md

This SSH command sets up a secure tunnel to forward traffic from your local machine to the Moltbot gateway running on the VPS. It maps local port 18789 to the gateway's port on the remote server, allowing you to access the gateway's web interface from your browser.

```bash
ssh -N -L 18789:127.0.0.1:18789 root@YOUR_VPS_IP
```

--------------------------------

### Expose Dashboard via Tailscale Serve (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/moltbot/moltbot/blob/main/docs/channels/googlechat.md

This command uses Tailscale Serve to expose the Moltbot dashboard privately to your Tailscale network. It supports binding to localhost or a Tailscale IP, ensuring only authorized devices can access the dashboard. The `--bg` flag runs the command in the background.

```bash
# If bound to localhost (127.0.0.1 or 0.0.0.0):
tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://127.0.0.1:18789

# If bound to Tailscale IP only (e.g., 100.106.161.80):
tailscale serve --bg --https 8443 http://100.106.161.80:18789
```
